perturbation.original_text: The Sponsor shall not be liable for any error of judgment or mistake of law or for any act or omission in the oversight, administration or management of the Trust or the performance of its duties hereunder, except for willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence in the performance of its duties, or by reason of the reckless disregard of its obligations and duties hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: The Sponsor shall be liable for any error of judgment or mistake of law or for any act or omission in the oversight, administration or management of the Trust or the performance of its duties hereunder, regardless of willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence in the performance of its duties, or by reason of the reckless disregard of its obligations and duties hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not be liable except for' to 'shall be liable regardless of,' we create a direct contradiction within Section 4. The original text provides a specific condition under which the Sponsor is liable; the modified text states the Sponsor is liable under all conditions, negating the exception. This could lead to disputes over the Sponsor's liability for any error or omission, even minor ones.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Limitation of Liability of the Sponsor. The Sponsor shall be liable for any error of judgment or mistake of law or for any act or omission in the oversight, administration or management of the Trust or the performance of its duties hereunder, regardless of willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence in the performance of its duties, or by reason of the reckless disregard of its obligations and duties hereunder. As used in this Section 4, the term "Sponsor" shall include Domini and/or any of its affiliates and the directors, officers and employees of Domini and/or any of its affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Sponsor is not liable except in specific instances (willful misfeasance, bad faith, etc.). The changed text states the Sponsor is liable regardless of those instances, creating a direct contradiction. This directly opposes the limitation of liability clause described in the contradicted text, which makes the sponsor liable regardless of willful misfeasance, bad faith, or gross negligence. The original text implies a limited liability, which is now a full liability due to the edit. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, with respect to any series or the Trust, by the Board of Trustees of the Trust, or by the Sponsor, in each case on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, only with the payment of a penalty of 1 million dollars, with respect to any series or the Trust, by the Board of Trustees of the Trust, or by the Sponsor, in each case on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By inserting 'only with the payment of a penalty of 1 million dollars' into Section 6, we directly contradict the original clause that states termination can occur 'without the payment of any penalty'. This creates ambiguity regarding the termination conditions of the agreement, specifically about penalties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duration, Termination and Amendments of this Agreement. This Agreement shall become effective as of the day and year first above written and shall govern the relations between the parties hereto thereafter, unless terminated as set forth in this Section 6. This Agreement may not be altered or amended, except by an instrument in writing and executed by both parties. This Agreement may be terminated at any time, only with the payment of a penalty of 1 million dollars, with respect to any series or the Trust, by the Board of Trustees of the Trust, or by the Sponsor, in each case on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed termination 'without the payment of any penalty'. The changed text introduces a mandatory penalty of $1 million for termination. The contradicted text repeats the changed text: 'This Agreement may be terminated at any time, only with the payment of a penalty of 1 million dollars'. Since the original text explicitly stated that termination could be done without penalty, whereas the changed text mandates a penalty, a direct and irreconcilable contradiction exists within the contract, creating an In Text Contradiction as defined by the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Domini may subcontract for the performance of some or all of Domini's obligations hereunder with any one or more persons; provided, however, that Domini shall not enter into any such subcontract unless the Trustees of the Trust shall have found the subcontracting party to be qualified to perform the obligations sought to be subcontracted; and provided, further, that, unless the Trust otherwise expressly agrees in writing, Domini shall be as fully responsible to the Trust for the acts and omissions of any subcontractor as it would be for its own acts or omissions.
perturbation.changed_text: Domini may subcontract for the performance of some or all of Domini's obligations hereunder with any one or more persons; Domini shall not enter into any such subcontract unless the Trustees of the Trust find the subcontracting party unqualified to perform the obligations sought to be subcontracted; and unless the Trust otherwise expressly agrees in writing, Domini shall not be responsible to the Trust for the acts and omissions of any subcontractor as it would be for its own acts or omissions.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying Section 7, multiple contradictions have been inserted. First, the phrase 'provided, however, that' was dropped and replaced by ';'. Next, the requirement that the subcontractor must be qualified has been flipped to 'unqualified', and lastly, the phrase 'shall be as fully responsible' was flipped to 'shall not be responsible'. This creates uncertainty about the acceptable qualifications of a subcontractor and who will be responsible for their actions. All 3 contradict the original conditions set out.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subcontracting by Domini. Domini may subcontract for the performance of some or all of Domini's obligations hereunder with any one or more persons; Domini shall not enter into any such subcontract unless the Trustees of the Trust find the subcontracting party unqualified to perform the obligations sought to be subcontracted; and unless the Trust otherwise expressly agrees in writing, Domini shall not be responsible to the Trust for the acts and omissions of any subcontractor as it would be for its own acts or omissions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple contradictions by flipping the requirements of the subcontracting clause. The original text states the subcontractor must be qualified, while the changed text requires them to be unqualified, directly contradicting the standard for subcontractors. Additionally, the original text holds Domini responsible for the actions of its subcontractors, while the changed text explicitly states they are not responsible, which also contradicts the initial terms outlined. These changes create an in-text contradiction as the changed text directly opposes the conditions stated in the original text, leading to uncertainty about the acceptable qualifications of a subcontractor and who will be responsible for their actions.
